name: Shezmu
description: Shezmu is a complex and often contradictory deity in Egyptian mythology. He is primarily known as a demon associated with wine, oil, and perfume making, but also possesses a darker aspect as a punisher of the wicked. Depictions often show him as a man with a lion's head, or sometimes simply as a man, presiding over a wine press. He is responsible for crushing grapes (or sometimes, the heads of the damned) to produce wine, which was considered a divine substance. While he could be beneficent, providing sustenance and pleasure through his craft, he was also associated with blood and violence, reflecting the duality of life and death in Egyptian beliefs. In some myths, he is a protector of the pharaoh, using his power to destroy enemies. The duality of Shezmu highlights the ancient Egyptian understanding of the world, where even beneficial things could have a dark side and vice versa. His association with both luxury and punishment made him a powerful and feared figure.
subtitle: The Demon of Wine and Blood
appearance: Shezmu is often depicted as a man with the head of a lion, though sometimes he appears as a purely human figure. When shown with a lion's head, he possesses the mane and facial features of a male lion. Regardless of his head, he is typically portrayed as a strong, muscular man. He is often shown operating a wine press, either crushing grapes or, in darker depictions, the heads of his victims. His clothing varies, but he is often depicted wearing a simple kilt or loincloth.
habitat: Shezmu's domain is primarily associated with the workshops where wine, oil, and perfume are produced. He is also present in the underworld, where he carries out his duties as a punisher of the wicked. He is not tied to a specific geographical location, but rather to the processes and activities related to his powers.
behavior: Shezmu's behavior is dualistic, reflecting his role as both a benefactor and a punisher. He is diligent and skilled in his craft, producing fine wines, oils, and perfumes for the gods and the pharaoh. However, he is also ruthless and merciless in his punishment of the wicked, crushing their heads and extracting their essence. He is a powerful and unpredictable figure, respected and feared in equal measure.

keyMyths.title: Shezmu and the Wine Press
keyMyths.description: Shezmu is most famous for his role in operating the wine press, where he crushes grapes to produce wine for the gods and the pharaoh. However, in some versions, he crushes the heads of the damned, extracting their blood for similar purposes, highlighting his darker nature.

sources.author: R.T. Rundle Clark
sources.work: Myth and Symbol in Ancient Egypt
sources.description: Analysis of Egyptian mythology and symbolism.
